Title: OPAQUE3, encoding a transmembrane bZIP transcription factor, regulates endosperm storage protein and starch biosynthesis in rice

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: ZJ100 (mutant analysis); ZH11 for overexpression in o3 background
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhonghua11 (o3 background); ZhongJian100 for mutant comparison
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Binding immunoglobulin protein (BiP), HSP70 family ER chaperone
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm; induced by ER stress and high temperature
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ER stress; high temperature during grain filling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderate overexpression of OsBiP1 in the o3 background partly rescues opaque endosperm and restores expression of ER-stress, starch-biosynthesis, and protein-biosynthesis genes toward wild-type levels; osbip1 mutant shows opaque/floury grains with 57-kDa proglutelin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBiP1 is a direct transcriptional target of O3. 2. Loss of OsBiP1 causes opaque/floury grains with proglutelin overaccumulation. 3. Moderate, but not excessive, OsBiP1 overexpression partly rescues the o3 phenotype, indicating dosage-sensitive contribution to ER homeostasis and storage accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-LUC transactivation; mutant phenotype analysis; overexpression rescue in o3 background; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive overexpression (300-900x) failed to rescue phenotype, indicating dosage imbalance may be ineffective
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (12-DAF/9-DAF developing grains; accession NA); ChIP-seq (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression promoter not clearly specified for OsBiP1 OE; native promoter in endogenous gene
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Moderate tuning of OsBiP1 could improve ER homeostasis and partially alleviate grain chalkiness/opaque endosperm caused by ER stress-related defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in protein folding/processing rather than as pathway enzyme or TF; rescue is partial and dosage dependent

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm; induced under ER stress/high temperature
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ER stress; high temperature during grain filling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: pdil1-1 mutants show opaque/floury grains with 57-kDa proglutelin overaccumulation, but PDIL1-1 overexpression in o3 background failed to rescue the o3 phenotype.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDIL1-1 is directly activated by O3. 2. PDIL1-1 is required for normal glutelin maturation/ER protein processing. 3. Unlike OsBiP1, PDIL1-1 overexpression alone does not rescue o3, suggesting O3 has broader control than this single chaperone.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-LUC transactivation; mutant phenotype analysis; overexpression test in o3 background
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression did not rescue o3 phenotype
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less suitable as a standalone engineering target than O3/OsBiP1 because overexpression alone was ineffective in rescue
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key ER folding factor affecting proglutelin processing; treated here as common gene rather than regulatory gene

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O3 directly targets and activates GBSSI expression. 2. GBSSI expression is reduced in o3 developing endosperm. 3. Reduced GBSSI likely contributes to lower amylose and altered starch properties in o3.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for amylose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates glucan chains using ADP-glucose to synthesize amylose within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature further suppresses starch biosynthesis-related genes in o3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amylose accumulation and normal starch grain development; downregulation in o3 is associated with reduced amylose content
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: O3 binds GCN4 and CCGTCC motifs in the GBSSI promoter
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly contributes to target product synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve affecting grain yield and eating/cooking quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be targeted with O3-centered regulatory engineering to optimize amylose and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter targeted by O3; reporter promoter used in dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qRT-PCR; ChIP-seq; yeast one-hybrid; dual-LUC; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered expression may change eating quality and starch physicochemical properties

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O3 directly activates AGPL2. 2. AGPL2 expression is downregulated in o3. 3. Reduced AGPL2 likely limits ADP-glucose supply for starch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major rate-limiting/committed control step in starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces ADP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature worsens reduction of starch synthesis gene expression in o3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation by supplying activated glucosyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target supported by ChIP-seq, Y1H, and dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for improving endosperm starch accumulation via upstream regulator O3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter targeted by O3; reporter promoter used in dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong manipulation may broadly alter grain carbon allocation

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O3 directly activates SBEI. 2. Reduced SBEI expression in o3 is associated with abnormal amylopectin chain-length distribution and starch-granule morphology.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branching step influencing starch structure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces alpha-1,6 branch points into alpha-1,4 glucan chains of amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature aggravates starch defects in o3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports normal amylopectin architecture and compound starch grain formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target shown by ChIP-seq, Y1H, dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms structural features of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch granule structure and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with O3-centered engineering for starch structure optimization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Structural changes may alter cooking quality and processing traits

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O3 directly activates ISA2. 2. Lower ISA2 expression in o3 likely contributes to defective compound starch grain formation and altered starch fine structure.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key starch-structuring step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes improper alpha-1,6 branches during amylopectin crystallization and starch granule assembly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature intensifies o3 starch defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports proper amylopectin structure and compound starch granule organization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional target of O3 per ChIP-seq/Y1H/LUC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper assembly of target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for coordinated starch quality engineering via regulatory modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter starch texture and granule morphology when perturbed

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding; ER-to-nucleus relocalization under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endosperm starch and storage protein accumulation, proper protein processing/export in ER, and ER homeostasis; mutation causes opaque/floury grains, reduced starch/amylose/protein, increased lipids, abnormal starch grains, 57-kDa proglutelin accumulation, and heat-sensitive grain defects.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded protein is OsbZIP60, a transmembrane bZIP TF with dual ER/nuclear localization; Leu139Pro mutation weakens transactivation; overexpression in WT showed no obvious phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes lower grain filling, reduced 1000-grain weight and grain yield per plant; manipulation may affect stress/homeostasis networks
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum and nucleus; predominantly nucleus under ER stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed, highest in developing grains, young panicles, and leaves; strong in developing endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter for complementation/GUS; UBIQUITIN1 promoter for overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; transmembrane bZIP (TMD-containing); OsbZIP60
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising master target to improve grain filling, starch/protein accumulation, grain quality, and heat resilience, but requires careful tuning because overexpression gave little benefit and loss severely penalized yield/quality
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsBiP1; PDIL1-1; GBSSI; AGPL2; SBEI; ISA2; OsGluA2; Prol14; Glb1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhonghua11; Nanjing11 used for mapping population
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ER chaperone genes and starch/storage-protein biosynthetic genes directly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress; DTT treatment; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Starch and storage proteins determine grain nutritional quality, filling, and yield
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsbZIP50; O3 homodimer (reported in cited work)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein biosynthesis; starch biosynthesis; ER protein folding/export; unfolded protein response homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O3/OsbZIP60 is the causal gene of the o3 opaque endosperm mutant. 2. It directly activates multiple starch-synthesis, storage-protein, and ER chaperone genes. 3. It relocates from ER to nucleus under ER stress and is essential for maintaining endosperm ER homeostasis, especially under high temperature. 4. Mutation impairs grain filling and storage compound accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing grains (HiSeq 4000; accession NA); ChIP-seq of GFP-O3 endosperm (HiSeq 2500; accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ER stress (DTT); high temperature during grain filling (35/28°C vs 28/22°C)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: starch; amylose; storage proteins (glutelin, prolamin, globulin)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central regulator/master regulator coordinating starch biosynthesis, storage protein synthesis, and ER homeostasis in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; MutMap; complementation; CRISPR knockout; qRT-PCR; GUS expression; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-LUC; phenotype and metabolite assays

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: ER-stress-responsive activation via unconventional mRNA splicing and cooperative transcriptional activation of UPR genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Assists O3 in activating ER stress-response genes, especially under high temperature and in o3 mutant background, contributing to ER homeostasis rather than directly determining starch/protein synthesis alone.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Spliced mRNA form accumulates in o3, especially under high temperature; known ER-stress regulator from prior work but experimentally supported here for cooperation with O3 in LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER to nucleus after stress-induced processing/splicing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by high temperature and ER stress; spliced form enriched in o3 endosperm under heat
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving ER-stress tolerance during grain filling, likely best used with O3-centered engineering
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances activation of OsBiP1 and PDIL1-1 together with O3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly preserves grain storage accumulation under stress by maintaining ER homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: UPR and ER protein folding homeostasis affecting storage product accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP50 expression increases under heat/ER stress. 2. Its spliced active mRNA form accumulates in o3, particularly under high temperature. 3. OsbZIP50 cooperates with O3 to activate OsBiP1 and PDIL1-1, helping maintain ER homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent ER stress regulator assisting the central regulator O3 under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; semi-quantitative RT-PCR of spliced/unspliced transcripts; dual-LUC assay; subcellular localization context from cited and current observations
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts cooperatively with O3 in transcriptional activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ER-stress homeostatic feedback loop
